name: Brew Tavern
address: Cabman's Shelter, 1A Queen's Rd, Nottingham NG2 3AS
notes: Staff was friendly, attentive & knowledgeable about their job. Plenty os seatings inside and out. Great location right next to train station. Love the selection of craft beers and they keep rotating with new stuff. Reasonable prices

name: King William IV
notes: A modern old-fashioned real ale craft pub for traditionalists, students, artists, locals, tourists and musicians. Basically everyone.

id: 15 minutes' walk from Nottingham Railway Station. Eight cask beers on handpump when I visited
name: including a stout plus a range of real ciders. Lots of outdoor seating upstairs
address: some of it covered. Bikes can be locked on the railing across from the entrance.,no,yes,yes,

name: Fox & Grapes
spot_a: Table on the left side of entrance
notes: Great pub with a light, airy feel rather than a dark traditional vibe. Plenty of seating too, with almost as much outside as inside, perfect for summer.

name: Neon Raptor
address: Unit 14 Avenue A, Sneinton Market, NG1 1DT
spot_a: Table on the left side of entrance
notes: Funky, hipster stuff. Good atmosphere and some unique beers.

name: Liquid Light
notes: The fantastic beers and pizza from Da Vinci Dough are a real bonus.

name: Bunkers Hill
notes: Watch the football on the massive screen. There’s a great selection of craft beers on tap, plus a fridge full of bottles and cans. The staff were really friendly too.

name: Lion At Basford
notes: Lots of real ales. The food is good and very popular.

name: The angel
notes: Cracking real ale/microbrewery pub. Pub food with GF and Vegan options. Quirky rustic decor and an alternative vibe.

name: Junkyard/kilpin
notes: Really cool place, great selection of, mainly Belgian, beers, lambics and ciders. Delicious food menu, the handheld section is especially good value for money. Lots of seating, outside, inside and upstairs.

name: Pitcher and Piano
notes: Lovely seating area to the side of the pub that is just visible from the road. get the sun most of the day.

name: Navigation
notes: Lovely quirky, interesting pub. Beautifully decorated and very clean. They have a small outdoor seating area onto the canal and a further courtyard accessed through the pub. The Navigation is dog friendly

name: The magic garden
notes: A fantastic space in central Nottingham with a really lovely atmosphere. The staff were very kind, the food was pretty good, and it’s a great place to be when the sun is shining.

name: Castle and Fothergills
notes: A wonderful old pub in an amazing location, with a good selection of local beers and great service.

name: Bustler Street Market
notes: Great location in the sunshine on a Bank Holiday Sunday.

name: Blend
notes: Friendly, happy place, broad mix of notts people and the food was brilliant. So quick, efficient and attentive service. Very popular so be prepared to share space.

name: Hopkinson
notes: Great £4 pints before 7pm. Great location by station and good outdoor seating. Sunny most of the day.

name: Embankment Pub
notes: Great Castle Rock pub with plenty of character, friendly staff, and lots of space inside and out. The covered outdoor seating is ideal for rainy days or shade when it’s sunny, and the Sunday lunches are excellent, with generous portions and good meat and vegetarian roast options. Best to book ahead, though walk-ins may be possible.

name: The Vat & Fiddle
notes: Nice pub with friendly staff, good outdoor seating, and a retro vibe inside. Handy spot near the train station.

name: The Bath Inn
notes: A stunning pub with quirky decor, where every corner and wall has clearly been thoughtfully designed.

name: Cured
notes: Views of the canal from every table and a small terrace overlooking the canal downstairs.

name: Cock & Hoop
notes:
Great pub, like a labyrinth inside and the outdoor areas are great. Very European feel to the terrace and enclosed garden space.
Very good deals on craft ales on Mondays.

name: Jamcafé
notes: Friendly staff and people, international clientele, great music really good vibe

name: Johnson Arms
notes: Friendly staff, a great selection of drinks, and tasty pizzas. The garden at the back is a beautiful surprise and a lovely place to spend the day. Definitely worth a visit.

name: The Boat Inn
notes: A cracking little pub with great aesthetics, a decent pint, and very reasonable prices. Big beer garden.

name: Broadway Cinema
notes: Great cinema to see films in. Lovely atmosphere. Lovely bar and food if required. Outdoor area is great for the afternoon sun.

name: Duke Of Cambridge
notes: Brilliant pub, great traditional warm atmosphere that's been lost in a lot of traditional city pubs. lovely beer garden and friendly staff. Good prices too.

name: The Canal House
notes: Great pub with nice indoor spaces, but the outside seating is the real gem, sunny, scenic, and yes, there’s a canal boat inside.

name: The Durham Ox
notes: Open Tuesday to Sunday, serving food and drink. Mondays are closed except for bank holidays where it is open all day

name: The Martin
address: School Ln, Colston Bassett, Nottingham NG12 3FD
notes: Outdoor seating · Fireplace · Vegetarian options
